title: Blue/White Screening of Bacterial Colonies X-Gal/IPTG Plates
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"dhq1q","text":"Dry X-Gal/IPTG-coated media in a laminar flow hood for approximately 30 minutes before use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"elrkt","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"buurm","text":"Plate Surface","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"68a5d","text":"1. Dry media plates in a laminar flow hood. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fqje5","text":"2. Add 40 µl 100mM IPTG and 120 µl X-Gal (20 mg/ml) to the surface of each plate and spread over entire surface.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7l032","text":"Note: The edges of the plate are difficult to spread adequately and may give false positives. We advise picking colonies in the middle of the plate, if possible, for best results.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"5sp48","text":"Prepare 20 mg/ml X-Gal solution in DMF (See X-Gal Stock Solution Procedure). For reduced DMF toxicity in media, you can alternatively make a 100 mg/ml X-Gal solution in DMF (this concentration is only stable at -20°C for ~1 week).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"56o40","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"62chc","text":"Prepare 100mM IPTG solution in dH2O (or dilute from 1M IPTG Stock Solution).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Cool autoclaved growth media agar to 50°C.
Add 10 µl X-Gal Solution (20 mg/ml) per 1 mL of Media (or 2 µl X-Gal Solution (100 mg/ml) per 1 mL of Media). 00:02:00
Add 10 µl IPTG (100mM) per 1 mL of Media for a final concentration of 1mM. 00:03:00 IPTG P212121 Catalog #GB-I2481C
Add screening antibiotic of choice (Ampicillin, Kanamycin, Carbenicillin, etc). 00:03:00 Ampicillin Sodium Salt P212121 Catalog #TWA-A-301
Pour plates and allow to cool to room temperature (usually at least 30 minutes) before use. 00:40:00

Spread transformed competent cells as desired. Note: Blue/White Selection plates are generally stable for only 1 week if stored at 4°C in clear sleeves, but may be stored in the dark (or a dark sleeve) at 4°C for up to 1 month. 00:05:00
creator: Sean Seaver
